title: tanacetum corymbosum subsp. daucifolium
intro: Tanacetum corymbosum subsp. daucifolium, also known as mountain tansy, is a plant species in the daisy family that is native to mountainous regions. It has been used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of mountain tansy include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory problems. Mountain tansy can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, although further research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential side effects.
